author: mysticryuujin
content:
Can you elaborate on how you think this would work? I don't really follow. You can pass it transactions and those txs have to be included in the block response, but it's not really clear to me what you're asking for the JSON-RPC to support?

Can you open a PR or suggest how it would work? What is being passed into the JSON-RPC request and what is the expected response change?

author: mysticryuujin
content: Even a mock example in an issue might help

author: jochembrouwer
content:
This suggestion: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/710/changes#r2944088614
Allow testing_buildBlockV1 to set the gas target for the block (so to increase/decrease block gas limit). It would move as fastest to the target (so within the 1/1024 increase/decrease rules)

author: mysticryuujin
content: Yes, I kind of understand the words but I do not understand how it would function in practice

author: mysticryuujin
content: What key/values/objects would be passed into the JSON-RPC body, and what would the resulting JSON output look like?

author: jochembrouwer
content: Ok right I see. I'll draft up a PR to explain

author: mysticryuujin
content: The method itself doesn't change the state of the node, it just returns some JSON output back that can be used
